title: teucrium orientale subsp. gloeotrichum
intro: Teucrium orientale subsp. gloeotrichum, also known as the Oriental germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium orientale plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Teucrium orientale subsp. gloeotrichum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
